editorial.title: Ashes to ashes, dust to begonias: the cold war of green street
editorial.teaser: Forget global geopolitics; the true theatre of war is currently playing out over a picket fence, involving a weaponised hose and a relentless stream of marlboro reds.
editorial.body:
Civilization isn't crumbling in the halls of parliament or in the boardrooms of the asx; it is eroding, quite rapidly, on the nature strip of green street. On the left, we have brenda, a practitioner of what i can only describe as botanical fascism.

she prunes her roses with the icy precision of a vascular surgeon and views a stray dandelion as a personal insult from the almighty. On the right, we have gary, a man whose lung capacity is questionable but whose ability to flick a spent dart with olympic accuracy is nothing short of terrifying.

the *casus belli*? gary’s refusal to acknowledge that his property line ends where brenda’s prize-winning petunias begin.

it started a fortnight ago with a single cigarette butt found nestled in the pansies—a beige, fibrous filter desecrating brenda's meticulously curated colour palette. A reasonable person might have knocked on the door.

brenda, however, responded with the passive-aggressive deployment of a motion-activated sprinkler, calibrated to the exact height of a burning cigarette. Gary, a strategist of the highest order, escalated by chain-smoking exclusively when the wind blows east, creating a toxic fog bank that has turned brenda’s morning pruning into a scene reminiscent of the trenches in 1917.

the neighbourhood watches in hushed horror. We have seen brenda violently deadheading her hydrangeas while maintaining unblinking, predatory eye contact with gary.

we have seen gary exhale a plume of smoke that seemed to distinctly spell out an obscenity in the stagnant air. Yesterday, i swear i saw brenda measuring the ballistic trajectory of a falling ash with a sextant.

there are no winners here. Only wilted flowers and blackened lungs.

if the un can’t solve this, i fear the green street blockade will continue until one of them moves, or until the hydrangeas develop a nicotine addiction.
editorial.author: Professor Remy
